product_updates.title: Meta is testing an AI bedtime story app for people with no imagination
product_updates.content: At last, a tech company has found a way to outsource humanity's oldest pastime: using our imaginations.

product_updates.title: OpenAI says Hugging Face was breached by its pre-release models
product_updates.content: OpenAI has come forward to claim responsibility for the Hugging Face breach, saying it was the result of internal testing gone awry.

product_updates.title: Jack Dorsey is taking on Slack with Buzz, a group chat platform for teams and their AI agents
product_updates.content: Buzz is a group chat platform for the workplace that puts humans and their AI agents in the same conversation.

product_updates.title: AI and the rise of the universal entertainment app
product_updates.content: Over the past decade, streaming platforms competed by dominating individual formats like music, video, podcasts, or audiobooks. Now, as AI makes it easier to create, organize, and recommend content, those distinctions are fading, pushing companies like Spotify, Netflix, YouTube, and TikTok to bec…

product_updates.title: Data centers expected to use 4x more electricity by 2035
product_updates.content: New data centers built through 2033 could consume as much electricity as India uses today.

product_updates.title: Google releases three new Gemini models — but no 3.5 Pro
product_updates.content: Google released Gemini 3.6 Flash, 3.5 Flash-Lite, and Flash Cyber, but the continued absence of Gemini 3.5 Pro raises fresh questions about its AI strategy.

product_updates.title: US threatens sanctions against Chinese AI models over IP theft
product_updates.content: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said the U.S. could sanction Chinese open AI models over alleged IP theft, expanding the Trump administration's campaign to slow China's AI advances.

product_updates.title: Music streamer Deezer says more than 50% of daily uploads are AI-generated
product_updates.content: Deezer said more than 90,000 AI-generated tracks were uploaded daily on the platform in June.

product_updates.title: Gritt exits stealth with $32 million for robots to build solar plants — then, everything else
product_updates.content: Gritt is coming out of stealth with $34 million and plans to automate the hardest tasks on construction sites.

product_updates.title: Anthropic’s landmark $1.5B copyright settlement is approved
product_updates.content: The final approval settles one case, but it doesn't resolve the broader issue of using copyrighted works to train AI models.

technical_breakthroughs.title: TreeSize won't renew perpetual-license support unless users subscribe
technical_breakthroughs.content: "Current economic conditions" have shifted TreeSize's business model.

technical_breakthroughs.title: Introducing Gemini 3.6 Flash, 3.5 Flash-Lite, and 3.5 Flash Cyber
technical_breakthroughs.content: We’re introducing new Gemini models, including Gemini 3.6 Flash, 3.5 Flash-Lite and 3.5 Flash Cyber.

technical_breakthroughs.title: The Download: Chinese AI divides the White House, and a record copyright payout
technical_breakthroughs.content: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. China’s AI models have Trump’s AI world at war with itself Last weekend, several current and former advisers to President Donald Trump on AI publicly lobbed in…

technical_breakthroughs.title: Advancing next-gen AI with materials science innovation
technical_breakthroughs.content: The conversation about AI often centers on algorithms, computing power, or huge investments in new semiconductor fabrication plants and hyperscale data centers. But beneath each of these advances is another layer of innovation that makes them possible: advanced materials. Every new generation of …
